title: Validating Git Ref Names with Special Characters

statement.markdown: Given a string representing a Git reference name, write a function that checks if the name is valid. A valid Git ref name consists of uppercase and lowercase letters, digits, and special characters such as hyphens, underscores, and dots. However, it cannot start with a period or a hyphen. Additionally, it cannot be empty and must be at least 1 character long.
statement.inputFormat: A single string input representing a Git reference name
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the input is a valid Git ref name
statement.notes: Consider edge cases such as empty strings and names containing only special characters

constraints.description: No constraint on the length of the input string

examples.explanation: A valid Git ref name with only letters and digits
A valid Git ref name with letters, digits, and dots
An invalid Git ref name starting with a period and a hyphen
An invalid Git ref name that is empty

hints.text: Use regular expressions to match the pattern of a valid Git ref name

editorial.title: Validating Git Ref Names with Special Characters
editorial.intuition: Use a regular expression to match the pattern of a valid Git ref name
editorial.bruteForce: Check each character of the input string against the allowed characters
editorial.betterSolution: Use a single regular expression to match the pattern
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression with anchors to ensure the string starts and ends with allowed characters
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression matches the pattern of a valid Git ref name
editorial.dryRun: Test the function with various inputs to ensure it produces the correct output
commonMistakes: Failing to check for an empty string
Failing to check for a string starting with a period or hyphen
